open_positions.name: Texas Roadhouse, Inc.
open_positions.thesis: no mechanism. Catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings + likely capital return announcement. Falsified by: n/a.
open_positions.latest_reasoning: Entry thesis: Q1 2026 earnings + likely capital return announcement on a LIKELY RATIONAL conf 4 flag with weeks horizon. Position -3.15% after 1 day. No contradicting news—earnings remain the catalyst and haven't arrived yet. Sector (Consumer Cyclical) weakness is real (housing data, appliance weakness in WHR/IBP) but TXRH is a restaurant operator (less direct housing correlation than builders/appliances). Hold for earnings catalyst; thesis remains on schedule. Down move is early noise on a multi-week setup.

closed_positions.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Original entry was OVERDONE conf 3 on 8-K surprise narrative. New flag reclassifies as RATIONAL conf 3 and provides 8-K URL (https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/927003/000110465926060543/aeis-20260513x8k.htm) confirming two consecutive 8.01 filings are 'likely investor conference / presentation materials'—benign. Thesis killed: the move (-6.13% new vs -7.96% realized) is pure sector beta (1.6x SMH), not an idiosyncratic catalyst. Position down -7.96% after 2 days with no directional edge remaining. Conviction gone; exit to redeploy capital.

closed_positions.name: Extreme Networks
closed_positions.exit_reasoning: Entered on thesis that EXTR was experiencing sympathy selling off CALX's -14% print—a short-term dislocations trade with 'days' horizon. 8 days in, EXTR has recovered +19% while XLK remains elevated, indicating the read-through trade has normalized and the catalyst (CALX weakness dragging peers) has exhausted its effect. Holding further exposes the portfolio to reversal risk without a fresh catalyst. The original high-confidence short-term mismatch has been arbitraged away. No evidence of broader enterprise IT weakness that would sustain further upside. Exit captures the gain and redeploys capital toward fresh opportunities.
